reading: decide-scaling probe. decide on a flat sum of K products is ~O(K^2) (Int time ratio 1000/200 = 1.0x, vs 5x if linear); Nat vs Int is marginal; term size is the killer. => the flat/'proof-term' PSD encoding is WORSE than the compact List-def form, and the ~N=60 kernel-PSD ceiling is fundamental to the trust model (kernel must reduce; native_decide forbidden). Breaking N>>60 is a charter decision (a new trust tier), not an engineering probe.
